yesterdayAnalysis: Monday's 1,500m pool swim with 4x50 race pace efforts was perfectly executed as the final pool session before Oregon. TSS 13, HR 104 avg shows excellent control and freshness. This was a sharpening session, not a fitness builder, and the conservative effort aligns perfectly with race week protocol.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's easy 20-min spin + 2mi run with 4x20sec strides exactly as planned. The strides are neurological activation, not hard efforts—feel fast, stay relaxed, and trust your fitness. Keep HR well under 130 on both disciplines.
weekProgress: Week 13 of 13: Currently at 33 TSS of 150 target (22%) with 5 days remaining including today. This is exactly on-track for race week's intentionally light, back-loaded structure. Mon/Tue sessions completed as prescribed—sharpening and neural prep taking priority over volume.
keyInsight: You're 5 days from race day with excellent sleep recovery (79 avg, 8.2h last night) and smart taper execution—trust the 13-week build and the Oceanside lesson: conservative bike pacing = run breakthrough.
